8	# WP Engine Account Data
9	
10	Topic: `$ARGUMENTS`

12	## Step 1: Get fleet-wide context
13

15	nexus wpe portfolio
16	```
17	
18	This shows all accounts, total installs, and fleet-wide visits + bandwidth.
19	Use this data to answer usage/traffic/storage questions directly when possible.
20	Only run additional commands if more detail is needed.
21	
22	## Step 2: Fetch additional detail if needed
23	
24	Based on `$ARGUMENTS` and what the portfolio output didn't cover:
25	
26	| Topic | Command |
27	|-------|---------|
28	| **Usage / bandwidth / visits (fleet)** | ✅ Already in portfolio above |
29	| Usage drill-down (per account) | `nexus wpe account-usage <accountId>` |
30	| Usage drill-down (per install) | `nexus wpe usage <installId>` |
31	| Prior month usage | `nexus wpe portfolio --month-offset 1` |
32	| Account overview | `nexus wpe account <accountId>` |
33	| Account limits | `nexus wpe limits <accountId>` |

41	| Go-live check | `nexus wpe go-live-check <installId> <domain>` |
42	
43	**If the portfolio shows zeros** for visits/bandwidth, the current billing period
44	hasn't rolled up yet. Run: `nexus wpe portfolio --month-offset 1` to get last month's data.

48	- Highlight accounts with the most traffic or storage

file.content:
---
name: nexus-wpe
description: Query WP Engine account data — sites, installs, usage metrics, bandwidth, visits, storage, domains, backups, SSL, users, and diagnostics. Use when asked about WPE environments, usage stats, storage, installs, traffic, or anything related to the WP Engine account.
argument-hint: [topic] e.g. usage, installs, domains, backups, users, diagnose
allowed-tools: Bash(nexus *)
---

# WP Engine Account Data

Topic: `$ARGUMENTS`

## Step 1: Get fleet-wide context

```!
nexus wpe portfolio
```

This shows all accounts, total installs, and fleet-wide visits + bandwidth.
Use this data to answer usage/traffic/storage questions directly when possible.
Only run additional commands if more detail is needed.

## Step 2: Fetch additional detail if needed

Based on `$ARGUMENTS` and what the portfolio output didn't cover:

| Topic | Command |
|-------|---------|
| **Usage / bandwidth / visits (fleet)** | ✅ Already in portfolio above |
| Usage drill-down (per account) | `nexus wpe account-usage <accountId>` |
| Usage drill-down (per install) | `nexus wpe usage <installId>` |
| Prior month usage | `nexus wpe portfolio --month-offset 1` |
| Account overview | `nexus wpe account <accountId>` |
| Account limits | `nexus wpe limits <accountId>` |
| All installs for an account | `nexus wpe installs <accountId>` |
| Install details | `nexus wpe install <installId>` |
| Domains | `nexus wpe domains <installId>` |
| SSL status | `nexus wpe ssl <installId>` |
| Backups | `nexus wpe backup-status <installId> <backupId>` |
| Users | `nexus wpe users <accountId>` |
| Diagnose install | `nexus wpe diagnose <installId>` |
| Go-live check | `nexus wpe go-live-check <installId> <domain>` |

**If the portfolio shows zeros** for visits/bandwidth, the current billing period
hasn't rolled up yet. Run: `nexus wpe portfolio --month-offset 1` to get last month's data.

## Step 3: Present findings

- Highlight accounts with the most traffic or storage
- Flag anything abnormal (high bandwidth, 404 accounts, missing SSL)
- Suggest follow-up if user wants per-install breakdown

content.text: I'll start by getting all installs from the portfolio, then check SSL for each one.

content.thinking: The CLI isn't authenticated with WP Engine. Let me check if there's a way to authenticate or list accounts. Let me try different commands to see what's available.

content.content:
Usage: nexus wpe [options] [command]

Manage WP Engine accounts and installs

Options:
  -h, --help                                           display help for command

Commands:
  accounts [options]                                   List WP Engine accounts
  installs [options] [account]                         List installs for an account (or all accounts)
  install [options] <installId>                        Get details about a specific install
  backup [options] <target>                            Create a backup of a WPE install
  cache [options] <target>                             Purge WPE install cache
  link <localSite> <wpeTarget>                         Link a local site to a WPE install
  changes [options] <localSite>                        View changes between local and WPE
  status                                               Show WP Engine authentication status
  login                                                Authenticate with WP Engine (opens browser)
  logout                                               Log out of WP Engine
  set-credentials <username> <password>                Store WP Engine API credentials for basic authentication (required for backup creation)
  clear-credentials                                    Remove stored WP Engine API credentials
  credentials-status                                   Check if WP Engine API credentials are configured
  usage [options] <installId>                          Show bandwidth, storage, and visitor usage for a WP Engine install
  account-usage [options] <accountId>                  Show bandwidth, storage, and visitor usage for a WP Engine account
  account [options] <accountId>                        Get details about a specific WP Engine account
  limits [options] <accountId>                         Show plan limits for a WP Engine account
  users [options] <accountId>                          List users for a WP Engine account
  user [options] <accountId> <userId>                  Get details about a specific user in a WP Engine account
  user-add [options] <accountId>                       Add a user to a WP Engine account
  user-update [options] <accountId> <userId>           Update a user role in a WP Engine account
  user-remove [options] <accountId> <userId>           Remove a user from a WP Engine account
  user-audit [options]                                 Audit users across WP Engine accounts
  sites [options]                                      List WP Engine sites
  site [options] <siteId>                              Get details about a specific WP Engine site
  create-site [options]                                Create a new WP Engine site
  create-install [options]                             Create a new install under a WP Engine site
  update-install [options] <installId>                 Update settings for a WP Engine install
  delete-install [options] <installId>                 Delete a WP Engine install
  backup-status [options] <installId> <backupId>       Check the status of a WP Engine backup
  backup-verify [options] <installId>                  Create a backup and poll until complete
  domains [options] <installId>                        List domains for a WP Engine install
  domain-add <installId> <domain>                      Add a domain to a WP Engine install
  domain-remove [options] <installId> <domainId>       Remove a domain from a WP Engine install
  domain-check [options] <installId> <domainId>        Check DNS status for a domain on a WP Engine install
  ssl [options] <installId>                            List SSL certificates for a WP Engine install
  ssl-request [options] <installId>                    Request SSL certificate provisioning for a WP Engine install
  ssh-keys [options]                                   List SSH keys on your WP Engine account
  ssh-key-add [options]                                Add an SSH public key to your WP Engine account
  ssh-key-remove [options] <keyId>                     Remove an SSH key from your WP Engine account
  promote [options] <sourceInstallId> <destInstallId>  Promote (copy) one WP Engine install to another
  diagnose [options] <installId>                       Run a diagnostic check on a WP Engine install
  go-live-check [options] <installId> <domain>         Check go-live readiness for a domain on a WP Engine install
  fleet-health [options]                               Show health overview of all WP Engine installs
  portfolio [options]                                  Show a portfolio overview of your WP Engine accounts and installs
  help [command]                                       display help for command

content.text:
The CLI isn't authenticated with WP Engine. You need to log in first:

```
node /Users/jeremy.pollock/development/wpengine/local-addon-nexus-ai/bin/nexus.js wpe login
```

This opens a browser for OAuth authentication. Once logged in, run the command again and I'll pull SSL status across all your installs — checking `nexus wpe fleet-health` for a quick overview and then `nexus wpe ssl <installId>` for any installs that look problematic.
